title: fagara rhoifolia subsp. pubescens
intro: Fagara rhoifolia subsp. pubescens, also known as the hairy Fagara, is a plant species that belongs to the Rutaceae family, commonly used in traditional medicine in certain regions. It is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and analgesic effects, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenes. The health benefits associated with the plant include relief from pain, reduction of inflammation, and treatment of various infections, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. The plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and topical applications, which are commonly used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
